title: Basic Timestamp Ordering Read Protocol
description: Pseudocode for the read operation in Timestamp Ordering protocol
explanation: This shows the read rule: a transaction can read a data item only if its timestamp is greater than or equal to the write timestamp of the data item. If not, the transaction must be rolled back.

title: Basic Timestamp Ordering Write Protocol
description: Pseudocode for the write operation in basic TO protocol
explanation: This shows both conditions for write: timestamp must be >= both RTS and WTS to ensure no stale read or overwrite of newer data.

title: Thomas' Write Rule Implementation
description: Pseudocode showing the modified write rule with Thomas' exception
explanation: Thomas' Write Rule allows more writes to proceed by ignoring outdated writes rather than rejecting them, improving concurrency.

title: Transaction Restart in TSO
description: Pseudocode showing transaction restart procedure after rollback
explanation: When a transaction is rolled back in TSO, it is assigned a new (later) timestamp and restarted, ensuring it gets a fresh chance to execute.

complexity.time: O(k) where k is transaction size
